Malveopsis is likely a misspelling or variation of Malva, a genus of plants in the mallow family (Malvaceae). Malva is a plant species with its roots in traditional medicine, known for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits.

The bioactive constituents of Malva include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
